text: When designing a REST API, following best practices helps ensure it is reliable and easy to use. Firstly, endpoint URLs should be clear and logical, using nouns like `/users` or `/orders` instead of verbs. Each endpoint should respond with the appropriate status code, such as 200 for success, 404 when a resource is not found, or 500 for server errors. It is also essential to version your API, for example by using `/v1/` in the URL, so changes do not break existing clients. Finally, thorough documentation is crucial. It should describe all endpoints, request formats, and possible responses, helping developers integrate smoothly.
